blocks.content:
Cassandra stood in front of her closet longer than she needed to.

Again.

That was becoming a pattern.

“What are you doing?” she asked herself quietly.

Her reflection didn’t answer.

Red hair pulled loosely back.

Eyes a little more awake than yesterday.

A little more aware.

She reached for one jacket.

Stopped.

Picked another.

Stopped again.

“This is ridiculous,” she muttered.

It was just a day.

Just time.

Just someone she had met—

someone she wanted to see again.

Her chest tightened briefly.

She paused.

Closed her eyes.

Breathed.

One.

Two.

Three.

The tension eased.

Not gone.

Never gone.

But manageable.
Then, almost defiantly, grabbed the first jacket she had reached for.

Because today wasn’t about perfection.

It was about showing up.
